sentence: Subjective:

HPI: The patient is a 45-year-old male with a history of diabetes type two and mild intermittent asthma, presenting with right elbow pain that started three weeks ago after increasing tennis activity.
He describes the pain as sharp, stabbing, aching, and throbbing, with a severity of 8/10 when using the arm and 3/10 when resting.
The pain is localized to the outside part of the elbow and does not radiate to the arm or shoulder.
He has tried taking Tylenol and ibuprofen, but they do not provide relief.
He also mentions that he has been playing tennis more frequently, which his doctor encouraged him to do to stay active and manage his diabetes.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of diabetes type two and mild intermittent asthma.
He is on metformin 500mg twice a day and flovent twice a day, and albuterol as needed.
He has a history of penicillin allergy and had his gallbladder removed 10 years ago.
Review of systems: The patient does not mention any other symptoms related to his elbow pain.
Current medications:
Metformin 500mg twice a day for diabetes
Flovent twice a day for asthma
Albuterol as needed for asthma
Penicillin allergy
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient has moderate lateral epicondylar tenderness of the right elbow, and pain with passive range of motion of the right elbow.
He also has mild pain with resisted extension of the right wrist.
The physical exam is otherwise normal.
Test Results:
Right elbow x-ray shows no acute fracture or other bony abnormality.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has acute lateral epicondylitis of the right elbow, also known as tennis elbow, likely due to overuse of the elbow joint.
Problem list:
Acute lateral epicondylitis of the right elbow (active)
Diabetes type two (active)
Mild intermittent asthma (active)
Plan:
I prescribed Ultram 50mg once per day for pain management of the right elbow.
I recommend that the patient rest his right arm as much as possible, avoid repetitive stress on the right arm, and ice the right elbow for 20 minutes at least 3 times a day.
I also prescribed a brace for the right forearm to help with discomfort.
I increased the patient's metformin to 1000mg twice a day to better control his diabetes.
I ordered a hemoglobin A1c level, CBC, and chem-12 blood test to monitor his diabetes.
I also wrote a prescription for a refill of albuterol.
Follow-up: I would like to see the patient in 4 weeks to reevaluate his elbow and adjust treatment as needed.
